alert.title: Alert
alert.description: A named alerting rule that evaluates a condition against observed signals (metrics, traces, logs) on a schedule and fires a notification when the condition is met. Represents the alert definition in the monitoring backend (e.g., Prometheus alertmanager, Grafana alerting, Datadog monitors), not an individual alert event.

severity.description: Alert severity level, controlling routing and escalation policy.

condition.description: Threshold or expression that triggers the alert when true (e.g., 'p99_latency_ms > 500', 'error_rate > 0.01'). Syntax is monitoring-platform-specific.

evaluationInterval.description: How frequently the condition is evaluated (e.g., '1m', '5m', '1h'), expressed as a duration string.

notificationChannels.description: Identifiers of notification destinations (e.g., Slack channel names, PagerDuty service keys, email addresses) that receive alerts when the condition fires.

description.description: Human-readable description of what the alert detects and why it matters.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.description: A named monitoring dashboard that groups visualizations of APM signals (metrics, traces, logs) to provide operational visibility into system behavior. Represents the dashboard definition in the monitoring platform (e.g., Grafana, Datadog, CloudWatch), serving as the primary observability artifact teams consult during incidents and reviews.

tool.description: Monitoring platform that hosts this dashboard.

description.description: Human-readable description of what this dashboard monitors and its intended audience.

panels.description: Ordered list of visualization panels on this dashboard. Each panel displays one signal or aggregation.

title.description: Panel heading shown in the dashboard UI.

signalType.description: The APM signal type visualized by this panel.

visualizationType.description: Visualization form used to render the panel data.

exporterconfig.title: ExporterConfig
exporterconfig.description: Configuration for telemetry data export destinations, specifying protocol (OTLP, Jaeger, Prometheus), endpoints, authentication, batching, and retry policies. Controls where observability data is sent.

authType.description: Authentication method used for the export destination

compression.description: Compression algorithm for OTLP export payloads. Applies to OTLP exporters only.

instrumentationconfig.title: InstrumentationConfig
instrumentationconfig.description: Configuration for OTel instrumentation of application code. Auto-instrumentation uses SDK contrib plugin libraries (e.g., opentelemetry-instrumentation-express) that hook into frameworks transparently at SDK bootstrap time. Manual instrumentation uses direct OTel API calls (Tracer.startSpan, Meter.createCounter) embedded in application code. This node controls which libraries or code paths are instrumented and whether instrumentation is enabled.

libraryName.description: OTel InstrumentationScope-compatible library name identifying the instrumented library or framework (e.g., 'io.opentelemetry.spring-webmvc-6.0'). Used to correlate this config with telemetry emitted under the matching InstrumentationScope.

instrumentationType.description: Whether instrumentation is applied automatically, manually, or both

instrumentationscope.title: InstrumentationScope
instrumentationscope.description: Named instrumented library or component that identifies the source of telemetry across all three OTel signal types (traces, metrics, logs), enabling attribution and filtering of signals by their origin.

attributes.description: Key-value pairs of observability metadata attached to this signal, per the OpenTelemetry attribute specification. Keys are strings; values may be strings, booleans, integers, floats, or arrays thereof.

logconfiguration.title: LogConfiguration
logconfiguration.description: OTel LoggerProvider configuration, covering the LogRecordProcessor pipeline, LogRecordExporter wiring, and minimum severity filtering for log emission.

minimumSeverity.description: Minimum OTel SeverityText level for log records to be emitted

logprocessor.title: LogProcessor
logprocessor.description: A processing pipeline component for log records, enabling filtering, transformation, enrichment, or routing of logs before export. Customizes log processing behavior.

type.description: OTel log record processor type (simple: synchronous, batch: asynchronous buffered)

logrecord.title: LogRecord
logrecord.description: Structured log record in the OTel data model, capturing an event with dual timestamps (timeUnixNano for event occurrence, observedTimeUnixNano for ingestion), a 1–24 severity scale, and an unstructured body. Supports trace context correlation to link logs to distributed traces.

attributes.description: Key-value pairs of observability metadata attached to this signal, per the OpenTelemetry attribute specification. Keys are strings; values may be strings, booleans, integers, floats, or arrays thereof.

metricconfiguration.title: MetricConfiguration
metricconfiguration.description: OTel MeterProvider-level (global) metrics SDK configuration, covering export intervals, cardinality limits, and exemplar filtering. Distinct from per-meter configuration in MeterConfig.

exportTimeoutMillis.description: Maximum time allowed for a metric export to complete

cardinalityLimit.description: Maximum number of unique metric time series per instrument

exemplarFilter.description: OTel exemplar filter controlling which exemplars are recorded

exemplarReservoirSize.description: Maximum number of exemplars retained per instrument per collection cycle. Used in conjunction with exemplarFilter to control exemplar sampling volume.

metricinstrument.title: MetricInstrument
metricinstrument.description: Defines a specific metric measurement instrument (Counter, Gauge, Histogram, etc.) with its name, unit, description, and attributes. The fundamental unit of metric collection.

type.description: OTel instrument type: synchronous (Counter, UpDownCounter, Histogram, Gauge) or asynchronous/observable (ObservableCounter, ObservableUpDownCounter, ObservableGauge)

source.description: Source code reference for code-defined metrics

resource.title: Resource
resource.description: Immutable set of attributes identifying the entity (service, host, process) that produces telemetry. Resource attributes are merged into all signals (traces, metrics, logs) emitted by a process at the SDK level, making accurate resource definition critical for cross-signal correlation.

serviceName.description: OTel Resource semantic convention: logical name of the service (service.name)

serviceVersion.description: OTel Resource semantic convention: version of the service (service.version)

deploymentEnvironment.description: OTel Resource semantic convention: deployment environment (deployment.environment)

attributes.description: Key-value pairs of observability metadata attached to this signal, per the OpenTelemetry attribute specification. Keys are strings; values may be strings, booleans, integers, floats, or arrays thereof.

span.title: Span
span.description: A named, timed unit of work in a distributed trace, as defined by the OpenTelemetry Trace Data Model. Each span is identified by a traceId+spanId pair, carries optional parent linkage (parentSpanId) for hierarchical trace composition, and is classified by SpanKind (SERVER, CLIENT, INTERNAL, PRODUCER, CONSUMER) indicating its role in the calling topology. The startTimeUnixNano and endTimeUnixNano bound the operation's timing window; statusCode (UNSET, OK, ERROR) is the canonical success/failure signal per OTel semantic conventions. Spans are produced via the OTel Tracer API, processed by SpanProcessors, and exported via SpanExporters.

spanKind.description: OTel SpanKind indicating the span's relationship to the calling and receiving system

statusCode.description: OTel span status code. UNSET = not explicitly set by instrumentation; OK = explicitly successful; ERROR = explicitly failed. Once set to OK, cannot be overridden to ERROR.

statusMessage.description: Optional human-readable error message, used when statusCode is ERROR.

attributes.description: Key-value pairs of observability metadata attached to this signal, per the OpenTelemetry attribute specification. Keys are strings; values may be strings, booleans, integers, floats, or arrays thereof.

spanevent.title: SpanEvent
spanevent.description: Timestamped annotation within a span's lifetime, used to record significant moments such as exceptions (OTel semantic convention: name='exception' with exception.type, exception.message, and exception.stacktrace attributes) or state transitions.

attributes.description: Key-value pairs of observability metadata attached to this signal, per the OpenTelemetry attribute specification. Keys are strings; values may be strings, booleans, integers, floats, or arrays thereof.

spanlink.title: SpanLink
spanlink.description: Non-hierarchical causality link to a span in a different trace or batch context (e.g., async message consumer linking to producer spans). Distinct from parent-child relationships, which are expressed via the parentSpanId attribute on the Span.

attributes.description: Key-value pairs of observability metadata attached to this signal, per the OpenTelemetry attribute specification. Keys are strings; values may be strings, booleans, integers, floats, or arrays thereof.

traceconfiguration.title: TraceConfiguration
traceconfiguration.description: OTel TracerProvider configuration covering sampler selection, context propagation formats, and span processor pipeline. Service identity attributes (serviceName, serviceVersion, deploymentEnvironment) are OTel Resource semantic conventions and belong on the Resource node.

source.description: Source code reference for trace configuration

samplerType.description: OTel SDK sampler type controlling which traces are recorded

propagators.description: OTel context propagation formats enabled for this trace configuration

samplerParam.description: Sampling rate for ratio-based samplers (trace_id_ratio, parent_based_trace_id_ratio). Value between 0.0 (sample none) and 1.0 (sample all). Ignored for non-ratio sampler types.
